title: So I decided to listen to the Commentary of Doom
display_title: So I decided to listen to the Commentary of Doom
wikitext:
[]: So I decided to listen to the "Commentary of Doom"             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 01, 2008 8:01 pm I've never actually listened to the English dub crew's DVD commentary for Death&Rebirth or End of Evangelion, so I took out the DVDs from my library today to see just how infamously bad it is. Plus I've never sat down and watched through "Death". Already did that without commentary; waste of time there; a few brushed up animation shots (i.e. Rei being freed from Entry Plug post-Ramiel), the Director's Cut scenes which were later added to the Platinum DVD's anyway, and the String Quartet thing which serves as an allegory to try to explain what the Human Instrumentality Project means. Plus the shot of Adam on Gendo's hand. Effectively, I think we can safely say people should just watch the Platinum DVD's (might as well watch the original episode 25 and 26 while you're at it" and then finish with "The End of Evangelion". Anyway, I'm listening to the commentary now....

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 01, 2008 8:06 pm Hold on now....this Taliesin Jaffe fellow claims to be the original "Eva Monkey"? What's all this, then? Well if he was the English script adapter for Hellsing and ROD he's no novice, must be an industry friend that didn't actually work on the show. Ironic how sometimes they got some voice actors back and some they didn't (i.e. "Black Hyuga") as they DID get Aoba (Jason Lee in commentary here) back but were unable to get him for the Platinum DVDs.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 01, 2008 8:09 pm ...ok there Taliesin....I'm pretty sure that Gendo, if he's "laughing at everyone else" would represent the Ego....not the moral "SuperEgo"..... Taliesin pronounces "Sachiel" wrong...

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Tue Jan 01, 2008 9:10 pm I too wonder about the Eva Monkey thing, though I vaguely recall the issue being adressed before. Don't blame me, I have a really bad memory when it comes to the goings-ons of internet communities. My 5 centes: I liked the dub pretty much, but whoever did Maya is the anime equivalent of Liv Tyler. I get really uncomfy whenever she's talking. I think Greenfield tied her up to the stove for the movies tho.

[]:             Quote Post by brainDamage » Tue Jan 01, 2008 9:15 pm Watch EoE with the commentary and you'll realize that all of the people contributing think they are kind of a big deal, but in reality, are kind of a waste of space and just distract me from the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 01, 2008 9:23 pm I actually think the voices they did were above average for an anime, among the best voices....but that doesn't mean they're like writers or something. I mean its nice what they've done for Eva but they act like "this is like the LOTR commentary and I'm Sean Astin!" and they're not. Not that its a problem, but much of it is just spent praising their production crew. ***Are you crazy? The original english Maya voice was kick-ass.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 01, 2008 9:44 pm okay, they spend most of the thing just giggling and offering little info. Interesting: they couldn't do all of the Eva noises just with people so for the Zeruel fight Eva 01 is supplemented with a warped screaming camel noise, in addition to the regular human snarling and growling. I still am not sure if they meant the Aoba guy did the Eva and Angel noises for just D&R, or for the whole series. And WTF, how can the String Quartet POSSIBLY be "officially in the real continuity, happening about a week before End of Eva"?! Asuka is comatose!

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Tue Jan 01, 2008 10:06 pm May be off-topic, but: The string quartet might not be in continuity, but I'd love to see it again.

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jan 02, 2008 5:47 am also kaworu is dead

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Jan 02, 2008 11:27 am I actually think the CoE is good for comedic purposes...

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Jan 02, 2008 11:43 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: I actually think the CoE is good for comedic purposes...

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Jan 02, 2008 11:56 am stop mocking Zugz, he'll bite you :P

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Jan 07, 2008 9:51 am I watched the comentary of doom thats actually how I got intrested enough toask myself "what REALLY happened in Eva?" I felt the commentary wasn't accurate and I t'was right the commentary cast does seem to have a bloated ego understatement of the millenium

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Mon Jan 07, 2008 9:58 am Does the theory of Kaworu being a clone of Ritsuko come up in Manga's commentary? I think I remember there was something like that in it...

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Mon Jan 07, 2008 10:11 am Dr. Nick wrote: Does the theory of Kaworu being a clone of Ritsuko come up in Manga's commentary? I think I remember there was something like that in it...

[]:             Quote Post by Sandoz » Mon Jan 07, 2008 7:10 pm My favorite bit from the CoD is when they mistake the angel statue Kaworu sits on for a tree. Trees don't have breasts, guys. Or wings.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Mon Jan 07, 2008 7:43 pm yes that was odd and I was left shouting "you brag you spent 4 months writing the script to make sure you got it right, but you frelled up the "Angels come from Adam not Lilith" line?!?!?! Does the theory of Kaworu being a clone of Ritsuko come up in Manga's commentary? I think I remember there was something like that in it...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jan 08, 2008 8:22 pm V wrote: Ironic how sometimes they got some voice actors back and some they didn't (i.e. "Black Hyuga") as they DID get Aoba (Jason Lee in commentary here) back but were unable to get him for the Platinum DVDs.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 08, 2008 9:53 pm Okay...for the record, we aren't considering the "Conglomerate Asuka in the Final Scene of EoE" as a "teach the controversy" thing like Evolution vs Creationism or what have you. "Conglomerate Asuka" is officially WRONG, because her eyes are NOT brown, the cell drawing is just reflection, etc. etc....we've confirmed all of this. And if it weren't for the Commentary of Doom, that idea would never get as widespread as it did. EVEN IF "celebrities" come to this site (ADV people, Weta Workshop people, whatever) we are going to politely but adamantly explain to them that this idea has no merit.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Wed Jan 09, 2008 12:33 pm V wrote: and I was left shouting "you brag you spent 4 months writing the script to make sure you got it right, but you frelled up the "Angels come from Adam not Lilith" line?!?!?!
summary: []: So I decided to listen to the "Commentary of Doom"             Quote Post by V » Tue Jan 01, 2008 8:01 pm I've never actually listened to the English dub crew's DVD commentary for Death&Rebirth or End of Evangelion, so I took out the DVDs from my library today to see just how infamously bad it is. Plus I've never sat down and watched through "Death". Already did that without commentary; waste of time
